seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Huberts Island and St Ives. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Huberts Island vs St Ives: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Huberts Island and St Ives.
heading: St Huberts Island vs St Ives — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Huberts Island, New South Wales and St Ives, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Huberts Island has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to St Ives (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Huberts Island or St Ives safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Huberts Island has a flood risk score of 40/100, while St Ives scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Huberts Island scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. St Ives scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Huberts Island or St Ives?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Huberts Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
